title: frenela australis
intro: Frenela australis, commonly known as the "Australian Frenela", is a plant species native to Australia, known for its medicinal properties. This plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Frenela australis include its use in traditional medicine for treating various conditions, such as fever, cough, and skin irritations. Frenela australis contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Frenela australis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, excessive consumption of the plant's leaves or roots can lead to gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to its toxins can also cause kidney damage and potentially lead to liver failure in severe cases. Furthermore, improper use of Frenela australis can also trigger cardiac arrhythmias, including irregular heartbeats and palpitations.
